name: City of Canton v. Harris
summary: A woman arrested and transported to the police station had seizures in police custody; officers provided no medical attention despite obvious signs of distress. The Court held that a municipality can be liable under § 1983 for a failure to adequately train its employees where that failure amounts to deliberate indifference to the constitutional rights of persons with whom those employees interact.
holding: Inadequate police training can support § 1983 municipal liability when the failure to train reflects deliberate indifference to the constitutional rights of those affected. Deliberate indifference in this context means the municipality was on notice that its existing training was inadequate — such as where the need for training in a particular area was so obvious that the failure to provide it constitutes a de facto policy choice.

legal_definitions.definition: The standard of culpability required to establish municipal liability for failure to train: the municipality must have been on notice that its training was inadequate in a particular area and consciously chose not to correct the deficiency, or the need for training was so obvious that failure to provide it constituted a de facto policy of deliberate indifference.
legal_definitions.context: Canton's deliberate indifference standard applies to the threshold question of whether a failure-to-train claim can constitute the 'official policy or custom' required for Monell liability.

relevance_to_audit: Establishes municipal liability for deliberate indifference to constitutional requirements in training; directly applicable to governments that deploy surveillance technology without training officers on constitutional limits or that ignore known gaps in compliance procedures.

key_quotes.text: Only where a failure to train reflects a 'deliberate' or 'conscious' choice by a municipality—a 'policy' as defined by our prior cases—can a city be said to have been deliberately indifferent to the constitutional rights of citizens.
key_quotes.context: Connecting the deliberate-indifference standard to Monell's requirement of an official policy or custom.

procedural_posture: Sixth Circuit reversed directed verdict for city; vacated and remanded
subsequent_treatment: Controlling standard for failure-to-train § 1983 claims; extended to failure-to-supervise and failure-to-discipline theories
